summary: Analysts at Chatham House and IISS have assessed that South China Sea militarisation is accelerating supply chain fragmentation for advanced manufacturing sectors including wind energy. State-linked actors are conducting sustained intellectual property operations against offshore wind technology developers across the Asia-Pacific. Expert commentary identifies APAC wind OEM exposure as materially elevated relative to prior assessment periods.
lead_indicators: IISS: South China Sea maritime law enforcement assertiveness increasing Q1 2026
Chatham House: APAC wind energy supply chain rerouting away from Chinese component sourcing accelerating
SANS Institute: OT-focused threat actor campaigns targeting APAC energy sector spiked January-March 2026

source_videos.title: South China Sea 2026: What the Escalation Means for Business
OT Security in Asia-Pacific: The Wind Energy Exposure
